title: Game Neon Genesis Evangelion Chapter 1 PS4 PS5 PC RUMOR
display_title: Game Neon Genesis Evangelion Chapter 1 PS4 PS5 PC RUMOR
wikitext:
[]: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Wed Nov 25, 2020 8:22 pm This reddit thread claims Bandai Namco is developing a bunch of games for the recently released PlayStation 5, as well as the older PlayStation 4 and PC. It includes this: Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 - Developed by CyberConnect2 (PS4, PS5, and PC)

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by Ambiant23 » Wed Nov 25, 2020 8:27 pm https://youtu.be/lMieDM5-vhg youtube vid about secret project. That said i am pretty skeptical that there is an evangelion game in developement. Although i want one sooo bad.

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by hui43210 » Wed Nov 25, 2020 11:15 pm This would be awesome... If true. I don't have confidence that it's real as of now, but it would make sense to give Eva a console game after so long, there's so much useless Evangelion merchandise, why shaft the video game market?

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Thu Nov 26, 2020 5:55 am If this happens I would buy all the games I wonder if steam will take on the series as their bringing over more and more Japanese games to the platform.

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by Joseki » Thu Nov 26, 2020 3:17 pm Reads like made up nonsense and CC2's speciality are over the top action cutscenes and shallow battle arena combat. There is basically nothing of that in NGE.

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by hui43210 » Thu Nov 26, 2020 3:42 pm Joseki wrote: Reads like made up nonsense and CC2's speciality are over the top action cutscenes and shallow battle arena combat. There is basically nothing of that in NGE.

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by Ambiant23 » Thu Nov 26, 2020 5:47 pm I kinda would like a vr game.

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by Blockio » Fri Nov 27, 2020 10:29 am Not to be overly cynical here, but I am almost 100% convinced that even though the name is "Neon Genesis", the game, if it exists, will be in NTE style. That being said - honestly I'd be down for an Eva game that isn't gacha or a glorified visual novel, and actually good

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by deeRez » Sun Nov 29, 2020 12:36 pm What we'll probably get is another Evangelion 64 or Evangelion Battle Orchestra game, but what's best for the series is one that's more character driven. I don't want to repeat the series exactly like battle focused games tend to do. I want to explore the world of Tokyo III. Walk the halls of Nerv in 3D. Give me something like Persona 5. I really liked what they were trying to do with the PS2 and PSP game Evangelion Another Cases. Unlike Persona games, there's no calendar in Another Cases, but it does count down to the Angel Event. While it counts down, the game plays like Persona but more like the Sims except you control only one character. It's like Animal Crossing in that sense without the furniture. The rest of the sims live out life, like asking for allowance money from Misato, or going to school. But after a while you realize how limited the AI is. I really wish they took Another Cases and expanded on it with more AI.

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Tue Jan 05, 2021 4:17 pm My wish for years now is for an Evangelion character action game similar to Evangelion Jo, where the EVA/Angel fights are all fast, flashy, dodge-heavy, Bayonetta style action, and the character segments where you control the pilot who's story you pick at the start, act as a hub world. Platinum Games' Astral Chain hit the mark almost exactly. Maybe mix in a bit of Daemon X Machina where if you run out of power you can choose to eject from your EVA and if you still somehow manage to beat the Angel you'd get a D rank, or if you just escape you get an E rank, completing the stage regardless, like running out of time in NiGHTS into Dreams. EVA Dawn on mobile does something a tiny bit like this concept, but as a 2D beat-em-up with a billion overly complicated upgrade systems you can pay to cheese. EVA so badly needs an awesome action game, best we have right now is mediocre-pretty good Smash clone Battle Orchestra. Hopefully once New Theatrical's story is complete, we'll start getting more NTE stuff. I'd love to wreck Zeruel-Rei, or do co-op missions on the L-Barrier Earth as Units 02' Beta and 08'

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue Jan 05, 2021 10:47 pm There is NGE2 for the N64, which at least to my understanding is more or less that, albeit dated and not as fast-paced as you talk about there

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 09, 2021 12:46 am Sighs and fires up PSP NGE2 in the meantime, while patiently waiting for the impossible

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sat Jan 09, 2021 11:51 am BTW, since this thread started, I've been checking out this Reddit thread. I don't think I've seen a single rumor posted on it that has been even remotely true. Seeing as the source of this was just some guy posting a list over a year ago, I'd say this game is non-existent.

[]: Re: [Game] Neon Genesis Evangelion: Chapter 1 (PS4/PS5/PC) - RUMOR             Quote Post by Zethus Thorne » Wed Mar 17, 2021 8:21 pm Maybe it wouldn't be a Retelling of the series like how most Anime games are but something in between 2.22 and 3.33 Maybe this is the game that moved the CEO of CyberConnect2 to tears during the development. We'll have to wait and see. Double post merged - staff
